name: Photoderm Spot-Age SPF 50+
hero: Bioderma's pigment-prone tinted SPF: light tint, antioxidant trio, and a moderate UVA-PF that puts it a step behind the long-UVA leaders. A pleasant tinted daily — but not the photoprotection champion the marketing implies.

scoreBreakdown.n: PPD ~28 is moderate. Falls behind UVMune / UV-Age Daily.
Vit E + ectoin + bakuchiol — small but real adjunct.
Tinted fluid evens tone, no visible cast on Type IV-V.
₹ 1,800 / 40 mL — pricey for the protection level.

ingredients.note: Modest in-vivo evidence as a daytime antioxidant.

useCases.b: Where iron oxides plus tint outweigh the moderate PPD.

useCases.k: Daily commute on lighter skin tones
useCases.b: Tint reads neutral on Type II-IV; cosmetic elegance is a strength.
Compact 40 mL bottle, photostable EU filters. Reapply often.

alts.name: Anthelios UVMune 400 SPF 50+
alts.note: Higher PPD; no tint. Pure photoprotection.

alts.name: Cleanance SPF 50+
alts.note: Mattifying for oily skin; UVA average.

faq.q: Is the tint enough for visible-light pigment?
faq.a: Yes for incidental visible light. Iron oxides at the level used here meaningfully reduce visible-light induced pigmentation in melasma trials. For severe melasma, layer over a higher-PPD base SPF.

faq.q: Does the bakuchiol do anything in a sunscreen?
faq.a: Modest. Bakuchiol's antioxidant data is real but cosmetic-grade in this context — it adds, it doesn't replace.

faq.q: How does it compare with UV-Age Daily?
faq.a: UV-Age Daily wins on photoprotection (higher PPD + niacinamide 4%). Spot-Age wins on cosmetic finish (tint). Pick by which problem is louder for your skin.

faq.q: Can I layer it over a vitamin C?
faq.a: Yes — give the C 5 minutes to sink in. The tint sits on top neatly.

sources.n: Lim HW et al. Visible light and pigmentation. JAAD 2016.
Castanedo-Cazares JP et al. Iron oxide sunscreen in melasma. Photochem Photobiol 2014.
Bioderma Photoderm Spot-Age in vivo dossier. 2023.
Chaudhuri RK et al. Bakuchiol topical antioxidant. Int J Cosm Sci 2014.
